title: XP & Persistent Leveling
problem: Player power comes entirely from gear drops with no intrinsic growth. XP and levels would give a persistent power-creep layer that survives death — acting like an extra equipment slot that scales up indefinitely.
analysis: MonsterDef already has baseXp field. combatStore resolves kills but discards XP. buildPlayerStats in combatStore computes stats from floor only — no player level input. gameStore has no xp/level fields. Level needs to persist across deaths (same as gear) to serve as the intended long-term power-creep layer. Level bonuses must feed into buildPlayerStats so the entire combat system respects them.
implementation: 1. gameStore: xp: number, level: number (both persist across deaths). 2. gainXp(amount) action: xp += amount, compute new level via levelFromXp() (level N requires N*(N+1)/2 * 100 XP), call set if level changed (+5 maxHp/level, +2 maxMana for level delta). 3. combatStore.handleVictory: xpReward = monster.baseXp * floor * tierMultiplier; calls useGameStore.getState().gainXp(xpReward). 4. buildPlayerStats: accepts playerLevel, applies +5 maxHp/level, +1 damage/level, +1 defense/level (Warrior +2), +2% critChance every 5 levels. 5. HUD: level badge + XP bar. 6. LevelUpOverlay: shown in CombatScreen on level-up. Displays the newly unlocked skill if one unlocks at this level. When no skill unlocks, shows a dimmed 'NEXT SKILL' preview card: the earliest skill for this class with levelRequired > current level, showing name, description, and 'unlocks at level N'. This helps players understand the skill unlock curve even when a level-up grants only stat bonuses. 7. CombatScreen: two useState values — levelUpSkill (the skill that just unlocked, or null) and levelUpNextSkill (the next upcoming skill). Level-up useEffect computes both: `setLevelUpSkill(unlocked); setLevelUpNextSkill(nextUpcoming)` where nextUpcoming = SKILLS filtered by classId with levelRequired > current level, sorted ascending, [0] ?? null.

decisions.decision: XP and level persist across deaths, never reset
decisions.rationale: Level is the intended long-term power-creep layer that survives death. Resetting level on death would make it indistinguishable from a floor-based stat.
alternativesConsidered: Level resets on death like floor progress
Partial XP loss on death (Dark Souls style)

decisions.decision: Level curve: total XP for level N = N*(N+1)/2 * 100
decisions.rationale: Triangular number curve naturally slows progression without a hard cap. Level 10 costs 5500 XP total, level 20 costs 21000 — meaningful grind without being infinite.

decisions.decision: Auto-distribute stat bonuses per level, no manual allocation in v1
decisions.rationale: Keeps UI simple. Manual allocation is a future feature that can be layered on the same XP system without breaking changes.
alternativesConsidered: Manual point allocation screen on level-up
Choose one of three random bonuses on level-up

decisions.decision: Bonuses: +5 maxHp/level, +1 damage/level, +1 defense/level, +2% critChance every 5 levels
decisions.rationale: Roughly matches what a good Magic item gives per level. Player feels like they are always getting stronger but gear still matters more at any specific tier.
alternativesConsidered: Only HP bonus to keep combat unchanged
Percentage bonuses that scale more aggressively

successCriteria: Killing a monster always grants XP visible in combat log. Level increments correctly at XP thresholds. buildPlayerStats reflects level bonuses in actual combat damage/HP. HUD shows level and XP bar. Level survives death and floor reset. LevelUpOverlay shows unlocked skill (if any) or the next upcoming skill preview.
knownLimitations: No manual stat allocation in v1
No XP from non-combat encounters (chests, shrines)
Level cap is infinite but untested above level 50 for balance
recommendations: XP formula should be visible in CodexScreen under a new PROGRESSION tab. Consider adding level to GraveyardEntry so the graveyard shows player level at time of each death.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: BMAD session 2: add next-skill preview to LevelUpOverlay (levelUpNextSkill state, nextSkill prop, dimmed preview card when no skill unlocks at current level)

publicInterface.name: gainXp

publicInterface.description: Current player level, persists across deaths

publicInterface.name: xp

codeSnippets.label: Level from total XP
buildPlayerStats with level
codeSnippets.snippet: const lvlBonus = { damage: level, defense: level, life: level * 5, critChance: Math.floor(level / 5) * 2 }

codeSnippets.label: LevelUpOverlay: next-skill preview when nothing unlocks

userGuide:
Every monster you kill grants XP. As you accumulate XP your level increases permanently — even dying does not reset your level. Each level gives +5 max HP, +1 damage. Defense per level depends on your class (+2 for Warrior, +0 for Rogue/Sorcerer). Every 5 levels adds +2% crit chance. Your level is shown in the HUD.

Max mana also scales with level, but the formula is class-specific:
- Warrior: 20 + level×4
- Rogue: 35 + level×6
- Sorcerer: 60 + level×6

**Level-up overlay:** When you gain a level mid-combat, a full-screen overlay pauses the fight. If a skill unlocks at this level, it is shown prominently. If no skill unlocks, the overlay previews your *next upcoming skill* (dimmed) so you always know what you're building toward.

Think of level as a permanent gear slot that keeps improving the longer you play.

codexEntry.title: XP & Leveling
codexEntry.summary: Every monster you kill grants XP. Level up permanently — even death does not reset your level.
mechanics: XP per kill scales with monster tier and floor depth
Level N requires N×(N+1)/2 × 100 total XP (Level 1 = 100, Level 5 = 1500, Level 10 = 5500)
Each level: +5 max HP, +1 damage, +1 defense
Level persists across deaths and floor resets — it is a permanent stat
Max mana scales with level (class-specific): Warrior 20+4L, Rogue 35+6L, Sorcerer 60+6L
tips: Level is your safest power increase — it never goes away when you die.
The crit bonus at levels 5, 10, 15 is significant — push toward those thresholds.
Your level is shown as 'LVL N' in the top-right of the HUD at all times.
